reasoning: Currently, there is no approved vaccine for Gonorrhea. The most advanced candidate is GSK's NgG vaccine which was in Phase II clinical trials as of mid-2023. Given standard regulatory timelines, this will certainly not gain FDA approval by January 2026. While the MenB-4C (Bexsero) meningitis vaccine shows partial cross-protection against gonorrhea, it is not officially recognized or licensed as a gonorrhea vaccine. The Wikipedia page for "List of infectious diseases" has an empty entry under "Vaccine(s)" for Gonorrhea as of October 2025. Without a formal vaccine approval, an enduring Wikipedia edit stating that a vaccine has been created is highly unlikely over the very short forecast horizons (between 7 and 90 days from the forecast date). Taking into account the remote chance of vandalism or a misinformed but sustained edit adding the meningitis vaccine, the probability is set exceptionally low.
